title: Everyone seems a little more
wikitext:
[]: Everyone seems a little more... [spoilers]             Quote Post by CATO » Fri Aug 07, 2009 10:37 pm ... healthy this time. If we compare this stage of Rebuild with its series counterpart, these characters are taking effective steps to overcome their issues. Rei is discovering her humanity in a more deep and meaningful way IMO. Asuka is showing a degree of reflection and empathy with others never seen before. Even Shinji is more determined than before, although his plot remains basically the same. And Misato seems somehow a little less self indulgent and messed up. Is Anno preparing another horrible end for his characters or something completely different this time?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Aug 07, 2009 10:38 pm I don't see why people like this show. Everyone's so healthy!

[]:             Quote Post by xeon » Fri Aug 07, 2009 11:27 pm Its a TRAP!!!

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:51 am It's all up for debate, and you can't really say. I'm sure we're all aware of Anno's status as God-King of Trolls, but which route is he going to take this time? Is he making these characters seem healthy and happy just to have them all die in such a grisly, horrible manner that EoE look like Dora the Explorer by comparison? Or is he expecting that we'll expect him to do this and make a bright, colorful ending where everyone frolics in the lollipop fields of the new, Imperial Japan lead by Emperor Gendo in a rainbow wig? Or he could just do another hour of still images. Regardless, we will all crap ourselves in awe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Sat Aug 08, 2009 1:56 am Considering how good they are doing the descent is going to be steep. Things better get pretty bad soon. Hopefully Anno won't pull a fast one on us and give us.....dare I say it...... a happy ending ....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 08, 2009 3:44 am I like to think our favorite fuck-ups being more well-adjusted is aiming for a happier ending (or not as bittersweet as EoTV/EoE)... Or it could be just like this image: "3 steps forward, 2 steps back!"

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Aug 08, 2009 4:07 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: "3 steps forward, 2 steps back!"

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Aug 08, 2009 4:21 am ^That's pretty awwsome! I'd like to second the request for a source.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 08, 2009 4:28 am I found it on THAT BOARD, so I'm not sure who the original artist is or anything. But yes, the image is indeed made of awesome and win.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:38 am I think, part of the reasons why the characters seem to be more open and sane can be traced back to the time constraints of the movie format. Character development had to be sped up, and presented in a more digestible format. Another part is that possibly the overall message and the main theme of Rebuild will be different from the original, and having the characters be presented in the way they are serves that goal better. I can't tell how Rebuild will and, but i'm prety sure the characters will face serious trials, pains and losses until the conclusion is reached. Because otherwise, well, it would be pretty boring. 


[]:             Quote Post by CATO » Sat Aug 08, 2009 9:49 am Sammaeloo wrote: Hopefully Anno won't pull a fast one on us and give us.....dare I say it...... a happy ending ....

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Aug 08, 2009 11:55 am Happier Anno = I don't need to spell it out.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Aug 09, 2009 9:16 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Or it could be just like this image:

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Aug 09, 2009 9:53 am Regarding the topic at hand: most comments i read here about changes made in characterisation almost universally hinge on preexisting knowledge from the original series. I wonder whether absolute newcomers who watch Rebuild as their first experience with Eva find anything interesting about the characters. Though i guess those who got into Eva through Rebuild are but a fraction of the overall viewing audience. 


[]:             Quote Post by DEMOLITION D+ » Sun Aug 09, 2009 10:19 am MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: It's all up for debate, and you can't really say. I'm sure we're all aware of Anno's status as God-King of Trolls, but which route is he going to take this time? Is he making these characters seem healthy and happy just to have them all die in such a grisly, horrible manner that EoE look like Dora the Explorer by comparison? Or is he expecting that we'll expect him to do this and make a bright, colorful ending where everyone frolics in the lollipop fields of the new, Imperial Japan lead by Emperor Gendo in a rainbow wig? Or he could just do another hour of still images. Regardless, we will all crap ourselves in awe.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Sun Aug 09, 2009 10:28 am regardless of if it's just anno trolling us or if he did, in fact, intend to make them healthier this time around-- i just love how our knee-jerk reaction is to go: "hey, who let all this sanity into my EVA?!" he does have us well-trained, that much is true.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sun Aug 09, 2009 10:31 am carla wrote: regardless of if it's just anno trolling us or if he did, in fact, intend to make them healthier this time around-- i just love how our knee-jerk reaction is to go: "hey, who let all this sanity into my EVA?!" he does have us well-trained, that much is true.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 09, 2009 12:05 pm trueno_twentyone wrote: EDIT: Happy endings for eva... can you cite an example of such?

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sun Aug 09, 2009 10:11 pm yes, we are watching the same series... and ok that was a good one, i just thought that it was a bit lonely at the end... but it really wasn't. I was hoping that RoE would have the end of Angelic days... which is very mellow, and somehow concreat for me. Because I felt that NGE ended a bit hanging, and EoE ended a bit..."almost there but not quite" for me.

[]:             Quote Post by DEMOLITION D+ » Mon Aug 10, 2009 12:06 pm ''
